revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hotaka jinja]][[en:Hotaka Shrine]][[ja:穂高神社]][[simple:Hotaka Shrine]][[zh:穗高神社]] {{Expand Japanese|穂高神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hotaka Shrine | native_name = 穂高神社 | image = Hotaka-jinja kaguraden and haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hotaka Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.338894|137.88428}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto|lt=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto|fr|Utsushihikanasaku|ja|宇都志日金拆命|qid=Q86736058|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}} | website = {{Official website|http://www.hotakajinja.com/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hotaka Shrine'''|穂高神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Beppyo shrine|lt=Beppyo Shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|mr|बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|simple|Beppyo shrine|zh|別表神社|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917303|ltq=Q134917303|qid=Q134917303|1=San-no-Miya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Q289250|ltq=Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181975 * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} Province}} ** Series ordinal: 7 ** P155: {{ill|Q62056170|ltq=Q62056170|qid=Q62056170|1=Arei Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Kawaai Shrine|lt=Kawaai Shrine|ja|川会神社|qid=Q85879982|1=Kawaai Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q534667|ltq=Q534667|qid=Q534667|1=Azumino}} * {{ill|Hotaka, Nagano|lt=Hotaka|ceb|Hotaka|en|Hotaka, Nagano|it|Hotaka|ja|穂高町|ko|호타카정|mg|Hotaka|simple|Hotaka, Nagano|qid=Q5910929|1=Hotaka, Nagano}} ** End time: {'time': '+2005-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} * {{ill|Q289250|ltq=Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} * {{ill|Azumi district|lt=Azumi district|ja|安曇郡|qid=Q11450769|1=Azumi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.338894|137.88428}}https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181975 == official website (P856) == * http://www.hotakajinja.com/ ** Mikoto|lt=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto|fr|Utsushihikanasaku|ja|宇都志日金拆命|qid=Q86736058|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}} * {{ill|Q1474187|ltq=Q1474187|qid=Q1474187|1=Watatsumi}} * Ómikami|hy|Ամատերասու Օմիկամի|id|Amaterasu|it|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|jaquote|アマテラス|jv|Amaterasu|ka|ამატერასუ|ko|아마테라스 okumiya|qid=Q135194118|1=Hotaka Shrine okumiya}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '本宮：長野県安曇野市穂高6079 奥宮：長野県松本市安曇上高地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長野県安曇野市穂高', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hotaka jinja}} [[EN:Hotaka Shrine]] [[JA:穂高神社]] [[SIMPLE:Hotaka Shrine]] [[ZH:穗高神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11597109}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Hotaka Shrine |Image = [[File:Hotaka-jinja kaguraden and haiden.jpg|280px]]
Precincts of the main shrine (Kaguraden and worship hall) |Location = Motomiya: {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Azumino City|Q534667|qid=Q534667|1=Azumino}}Hotaka 6079
Okumiya: Nagano Prefecture{{ill|lt=Matsumoto City|Q213324|qid=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}}Azumi Kamikochi |ISO = JP-20 |Position = Main shrine: {{Coord|36|20|19.2|N|137|53|3.5|E|display=inline,title}}
Oku shrine: {{Coord|36|15|12.3|N|137|39|51.6|E|display=inline}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Utsushi Nikkine no Mikoto|Q86736058|qid=Q86736058|lt=Hotakami no Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi no Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}}
{{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Qiongokuki no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} |Shintai = {{ill|lt=Mt. Hotaka|Q656026|qid=Q656026|1=Mount Hotakadake}} ({{ill|lt=Mt.Shintai|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}) |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
{{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
Old {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system #government shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kokuhei-kosha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |Foundation = unknown|Main shrine = Hotakazo |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = Main Shrine: {{ill|lt=September 26th|Q2885|qid=Q2885|1=September 26}}・{{ill|lt=September 27th|Q2884|qid=Q2884|lt=27th|1=September 27}} (Mifune Festival)
Okumiya: {{ill|lt=October 8th|Q2924|qid=Q2924|1=October 8}} |Shinto ritual = Sengu Festival (Shikinen Festival)
Hosha Festival ({{ill|lt=March 17th|Q2419|qid=Q2419|1=March 17}}) |Map = {{Location map+|Japan Nagano|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|coordinates= {{coord2|36|20|19.2|N|137|53|3.5|E}}|position=right|label=Motomiya|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|coordinates= {{coord2|36|15|12.3|N|137|39|51.6|E}}|position=bottom|label=Okumiya|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}}{{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|36.303|N|137.759|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=9|float=center|width=202|height=250|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|36|20|19.2 |N|137|53|3.5|E}}|label1=Motomiya|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|36|15|12.3|N|137|39|51.6|E}}|label2=Okumiya|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Mt.Hotaka from Mt.Otenshodake 01.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Hotakadake|Q656026|qid=Q656026|1=Mount Hotakadake}} (ceremonial object)}}]] [[File:Hotaka-jinja torii-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]``Hotaka Shrine'' (Hotaka Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Azumino City|Q534667|qid=Q534667|1=Azumino}}{{ill|lt=Hotaka (Azumino City)|Q115648439|qid=Q115648439|lt=Hotaka|1=Hotaka}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinai Shrine|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}. {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System#Government Shrine|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kokuhei-kosha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and currently it is {{ill|lt=Beppedo Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Shinto Honcho|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In addition to the main shrine (satomiya) in Hotaka, Azumino City, there is also Myojin Pond in {{ill|lt=Kamikochi|Q1048234|qid=Q1048234|1=Kamikōchi}} in {{ill|lt=Matsumoto City|Q213324|qid=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} in Azumino. (Matsumoto City)|Q11512587|Okunomiya}}, {{ill|lt=Mt. Hotaka|Q656026|qid=Q656026|lt=Mt. Okuhotaka|1=Mount Hotakadake}} Because there is a shrine at the top of Mt., it is commonly known as ``the total guardian of the {{ill|lt=Japanese Alps|Q645773|qid=Q645773|1=Japanese Alps}}.'' Also, the annual festival (Mifune Festival) held every year on {{ill|lt=September 27th|Q2884|qid=Q2884|1=September 27}} is famous. === Enshrined God === The deities enshrined at the main shrine are as follows. The main enshrined deity is in bold. * Chudono: '''{{ill|lt=Utsushi Nikkin Kin-no-Mikoto|Q86736058|qid=Q86736058|lt=Hotakami-no-Mikoto|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}''' (Hotakami no Mikoto) - Another name is "Utsushi Nikin Kin-no-Mikoto." Child of Watatsumi no Mikoto. * Sadono: '''{{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi no Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}}''' (Watatsumi no Mikoto) - The god of the sea and the ancestor of the Azumi clan. * Right Hall: '''{{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Qiongiongi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}''' (Ninigi no Mikoto) * Betsugu: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}} (Amaterasu Omikami) * Wakamiya: {{ill|lt=Azumi Hirafu|UNKNOWN|lt=Azumi Renhirafu no Mikoto}} (Azumi Muraji Hirafu Mikoto) ** Wakamiya Aiden: Lieutenant General Shinano (Shinano Chusho) - Said to be the model for {{ill|lt=Monokusa Taro|Q17219533|qid=Q17219533|lt=Monogusa Taro|1=Monogusa Taro}} in {{ill|lt=Otogi Zoshi|Q82440|qid=Q82440|1=Otogi-zōshi}}. === About the enshrined deity === Since ancient times, the area around Hotaka Shrine has been known as the settlement area of the {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|qid=Q3377806|lt=Azumi|1=Azumi people}} (accurately speaking, currently only the Azumi clan has been confirmed) and the Azumi Inukai clan , and can also be seen as the name of Azumi {{ill|lt=gunji|Q4152149|qid=Q4152149|1=Gunji}} in the Nara period. There is {{ill|lt=Azumino|Q4833082|qid=Q4833082|1=Azumi Basin}} as a place name around the shrine. The following three articles are known to be related to the Azumi clan in "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}" "History and Landscape Hotaka Shrine" pp. 2-3. . * Ukyojinbetsu Azumi Sukunejo - After Hodakami no Mikoto, the son of {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|qid=Q1474187|lt=sea god Watatsumi Toyotamahikokami|1=Watatsumi}}. * Ukyojinbetsu Bonkairenjo - After Hodakami no Mikoto, the son of Kaijin Watazumi no Mikoto. * Kawachi no Kuni Kamibetsu Azumi Renjo - After the son of the sea god Watazumi no Mikoto, Hotakami no Mikoto.The Hodaka clan, {{ill|lt=shake|Q11588367|qid=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} of Hotaka Shrine, is a descendant of Hotakami no Mikoto, and the theory that the deity enshrined at Hotaka Shrine is ``Hotakami no Mikoto'' is based on the relationship between the company name, these historical materials, and the history of the shrine and its surroundings. Furthermore, in the Jindaiki "{{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}}", there is a mention of "{{ill|lt=Utsushi Nikkiniki-no-Mikoto|Q86736058|qid=Q86736058|1=Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto}}" as the son of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumi-sanjin|1=UNKNOWN}} (Watatsumi-kami and Watazumi-kami) and the founder of the Azumi clan, but another name for this god is said to be Hotakamino-Mikoto.. Other children of Watatsumi no Mikoto include {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama Bime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the wife of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikohihodemi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{jalink|Tamayoribime, the wife of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ubuki Kusabuki Fugyou no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} and the mother of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|qid=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. (Hyuga mythology)|Q3082751|Tamayibime no Mikoto}} exists. On the other hand, there is a theory that the company name ``Hotaka Shrine'' simply means ``the shrine of the gods of Hotaka'' or ``the shrine that sits in Hotaka'' . In addition, in ancient times, the enshrined deity was one troupe (in the Engishiki Shinmyocho), but in the Middle Ages there were three shrines: Omiya, Minamigu, and Wakamiya, and in a document dated 18 ({{ill|lt=1549|Q6554|qid=Q6554|1=1549}}), Gosho Daimyojin (deity unknown) was also referred to as Gosho (deity unknown).Hotaka Shrine section of "Place name of Nagano prefecture". . === History === === Founding === [[File:Shikaumi-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Shigakai Shrine|Q11491171|qid=Q11491171|1=Shikaumi Shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Fuku Oka Prefecture|Q123258|qid=Q123258|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Fukuoka City|Q26600|qid=Q26600|1=Fukuoka}})}}{{Small|{{ill|lt=Azumi Clan|Q3377806|qid=Q3377806|1=Azumi people}} (Azumi Clan) birthplace. }}]] Founding date is unknown. The enshrined area is in the {{ill|lt=Azumi County|Q11450769|qid=Q11450769|1=Azumi district}} district, and it is thought to have been established by the established {{ill|lt=Azumi clan|Q3377806|qid=Q3377806|lt=Azumbe clan|1=Azumi people}}, and it is said that the ancestral god was enshrined by the Azumbe clan. There are various theories about how the Azumi clan settled in Azumi District (for details, see "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Azumi clan#Distribution of the Azumi clan|1=UNKNOWN}}"). There is also {{ill|lt=Kawaai Shrine|Q85879982|qid=Q85879982|1=Kawaai Shrine}} in {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} in Azumi District, but here too {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Watatsumijin|1=UNKNOWN}} of the Azumi clan is said to be the enshrined deity . Many ancient tombs were built to the west of Hotaka Shrine, but the area around Hotaka Shrine is thought to have been avoided as a sacred area, and the area around Hotaka Shrine is thought to have been the center of power.The first sight of the Azumi clan in Azumi District is an ink calligraphy dated {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Houji|Q1346307|qid=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}}8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|qid=Q31163|1=764}}) found on a {{ill|lt=Shosoin|Q2379007|qid=Q2379007|1=Shōsōin}} treasured cloth hakama. However, since there is only a mention of ``Azumi-bu'' here, there is a theory that Mr. Azumi's {{ill|lt=bu-kyoku|Q5046108|qid=Q5046108|1=bu-kyoku}} was simply installed in this area. . === Outline === The first appearance of the document is that on February 11, {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), ``Hotakujin'' was promoted {{ill|lt=Divine grade|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper.``Japanese Gods'' Hotaka Shrine section. . In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, "Hotaka Shrine It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. Also, as seen in the ``Sannomiya Hotakasha Gozogu Jo Nikki'' written in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|qid=Q6736|1=1585}}), Sannomiya of Shinano Province was called ``Japanese Medieval States and the Ichinomiya System of the Provinces'' (Iwata Shoin) p. 55. .When the {{ill|lt=Nishina clan|Q11376452|qid=Q11376452|1=Nishina clan}}, the local feudal lord who had ruled the Azumi district since the Middle Ages, fell, Azumi district became the territory of the {{ill|lt=Matsumoto Castle|Q739612|qid=Q739612|lt=Lord of Matsumoto Castle|1=Matsumoto Castle}}, and in 10 years ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} {{ill|lt=Sadayoshi Ogasawara|Q10856562|qid=Q10856562|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi}}, who became the lord of the castle in 1582|Q6719}}, donated the land as a divine territory, and from {{ill|lt=Hidemasa Ogasawara|Q8192119|qid=Q8192119|1=Ogasawara Hidemasa}} passed it down from generation to generation, striving to carry out shrine transfers and rituals in ceremonial years.. In ancient times, it was influenced by Suwa Taisha Shrine, and according to the "Jijiki" there was a south shrine in the precincts that enshrined the Suwa god, and there was also a custom of erecting pillars. It was also strongly influenced by Buddhism, and according to documents, there was a Jingu-ji temple within the precincts in the Middle Ages, and in the Edo period, there was a Yakushi-do hall to the southwest of the main hall, which served as the main hall . During the {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|qid=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} years ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|qid=Q6769|1=1592}} - {{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|qid=Q6785|1=1596}}), 15 koku of red seals were enshrined at Hotaka Shrine and 3 koku of red seals were enshrined at Jingu-ji Temple . The Shinto priest was the Azumi clan in ancient times, but in the medieval period it was held by the {{ill|lt=Otomo clan|Q17227938|qid=Q17227938|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}, and was later replaced by the Nishina clan.After entering {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, in the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, and in the 15th year of Meiji ({{ja link|1882|Q7812}}), it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}, and in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}15 ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|qid=Q18633|1=1940}}), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system #government company|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kokuheishosha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} February 11, 3rd year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jironki|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - The notation is ``Hotakujin''. === Precincts === === Motomiya (Satomiya) === [[File:Hotaka-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|main hall}}{{Small|The style of the middle hall is called ``Hotaka-zukuri''. }}]] The main shrine has a three-dono structure, with the right shrine, middle shrine, and left shrine all having the same format, and these three shrines are lined up with the smaller Shinmeisha Shrine. Among these, the central shrine has a unique form of ``Chigi'' and ``Katsuogi'' that are passed down only to Hotaka Shrine. Katsuogi is said to represent a fishing rod or a ship's mast, and this form is called ``Hotaka-zukuri.''History and Landscape Hotaka Shrine, p. 4. . At the Sengu Festival held in Shikinen, the shrine buildings are remodeled and rearranged according to the method specified in the ``Jijiki.'' In the precincts, the Kagura Hall stands in front of the worship hall, and to the right of it are Wakamiya Shrine and several shrine shrines. In addition, the hand-washing stone, the chozuya, and the Shinbashi bridge are designated cultural properties by Azumino City, and the Daimon zelkova and Wakamiya Nishi zelkova are designated natural monuments by Azumino City. Hotaka Shrine is listed in the ``Zenkoji Domeisho Zue'' of 14 years ({{ill|lt=1843|Q7629|qid=Q7629|1=1843}}), which gives us an idea of ​​what the precincts looked like at that time. File:150921 Hotaka-jinja Azumino Nagano pref Japan04n.jpg|Hotel File:Hotaka-jinja Kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden File:Hotaka-jinja Wakamiyasha-2.jpg|WakamiyashaFile:Hotaka-jinja Shinboku.jpg|Wakamiya Nishi Keyaki (Azumino City designated natural monument) File:Hotaka-jinja shinkyo-2.jpg|Shinkyo Bridge (Azumino City designated cultural property) File:Hotaka-jinja Hotaka-maru-2.jpg|God ship "Hotaka Maru" File:Hotaka-jinja chozusha.jpg|Chozusha (cultural property designated by Azumino City) File:Hotaka-jinja ootorii-2.jpg|Otorii === Okumiya === [[File:150920 Hotaka-jinja Okumiya Kamikochi Japan03n.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Okumiya Haisho}}]] Okumiya is located in Kamikochi, Azumi, Matsumoto City. It rests at the entrance of {{ill|lt=Myojin Pond (Matsumoto City)|Q11512587|qid=Q11512587|lt=Myojin Pond|1=Myōjin Pond}} in {{ill|lt=Chubu Sangaku National Park|Q764357|qid=Q764357|1=Chūbu-Sangaku National Park}}. Myojin Pond, a scenic spot next to Okumiya, is within the grounds of the same shrine and is {{ill|lt=sacred area|Q11588853|qid=Q11588853|1=shin'iki}}. The enshrined deity is Hotakami no Kami, and the main shrine is built in Hotakazukuri. Unlike the main shrine, the Mifune ritual at Okumiya is held on October 8th every year by floating a boat on Myojin Pond. Additionally, the opening festival will be held on April 27th and the closing festival will be held on November 15th. The name ``Kamikochi'' (Kamikochi, Kamigaki) is said to have come from the fact that the Hotaka Shrine Okumiya, which enshrines the deity Hotakami, and Myojin Pond are located here. === Minemiya === Minegu Shrine is enshrined at the top of Mt. Okuhotakadake (3,190{{ill|lt=meter|Q11573|qid=Q11573|1=Metre}}), where the god Hotakami is said to have descended ({{Coord|36|1 7|21.14|N|137|38|52.81|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=Reimiya (top of Mt. Okuhotakadake)}}). The shrine building is a small shrine made of white stone. === Setsusha === [[File:150921 Hotaka-jinja Azumino Nagano pref Japan06n.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Hongu Precinct Shrine}}]] * Kashimasha * Yahata Shrine * Akibasha * Pest Shrine * Four shrines *Hoshokusha * Koyasusha * Kohirasha * Yasakasha * Uta Shrine * Sugawarasha * Hachiojisha * Suwasha - Relocated from a private residence. * Itsukushimasha *Hotakareisha Shrine === Festival === === Shikinen Festival === As part of the Shikinen Festival, the ``Daisaku Sengu Festival'' is held once every 20 years, in which the entire main shrine building is rebuilt, and the ``Small Sengu Festival'', in which repairs are carried out twice during that period. The festival is held in an old style that dates back to before 1483, according to the ``Sannomiya Hotaka Shrine Gozogu Jo Nikki'' (a cultural property designated by Azumino City) handed down at Hotaka Shrine, and has been designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Azumino City. The Shinto ritual begins with the Shishisakakitate Shinto ritual 100 days before the transfer. Once the main shrines had finished their functions, they were given away to each company or demolished, and then relocated, and only about a dozen buildings still exist today. === Annual festival === * Saidansai, long live Seiju, prayers for the safety of parishioners and worshipers (January 1st) * Grand Peace and Traffic Safety Prayer Festival (January 1st - 8th) * Special prayer festival for warding off evil spirits and warding off evil spirits (January Coming of Age Day) * Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Prayer Festival, Hosha Festival (special ritual), prayer for the promotion of agriculture, industry, commerce, and industry (March 17th) * Kangakusai (last Sunday in March) * War Memorial Festival (Peace Prayer Festival) (April 20th) * Great Purification Ceremony (Prayer to ward off misfortune) (June 30th) * Ancestry Festival (August 2nd) * Hongu Reitaisai (September 26th - 27th) ** Yoimiya (September 26th) ** Main festival (September 27) - Mifune Shinto ritual (designated intangible folk cultural property by Nagano Prefecture). * Okumiya Festival (October 8th) * Shinjosai, Reikaku Anniversary Festival (November 19th) * Great Purification Ceremony, New Year's Eve Festival (December 31st) === Mifune Festival === [[File:Hotaka-jinja ofune.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mifune}}]] The annual festival is called ``''Mifune Festival'' and is held on September 26th and 27th every year. It is a heroic festival where large boat-shaped {{ill|lt=dashi|Q11470974|qid=Q11470974|1=Matsuri float}} (dashi) ``Ofune'' (dashi), which are 6 meters high and 12 meters long, are collided with each other, and has been designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Nagano Prefecture. The 26th is only a Shinto ritual, and the main festival is on the 27th. September 27th is said to be the death anniversary of Hirafu Azumi, who was killed in the {{ill|lt=Battle of Hakumurae|Q608727|qid=Q608727|1=Battle of Baekgang}} in the second year of Emperor Tenji ({{ill|lt=663|Q31122|qid=Q31122|1=663}}). During the festival, five large and small boats are decorated with Hotaka dolls and {{ill|lt=parishioners|Q125562175|qid=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} parade the boats through the streets of Hotaka and the countryside. They are then towed to the shrine, paraded around the Kagura hall within the shrine grounds, and as they are towed around the shrine, the two large ships violently collide with each other. It attracts many visitors due to its power and splendor. There is also a ``Children's Festival'' held on the first Saturday and Sunday of September, where children's boats are paraded through the town. The Mifune used in the Mifune ritual is on display at the Mifune Kaikan (admission fee: 300 yen for adults) on the north side of the main shrine. The structure of the Mifune consists of a turret (elongated cube), four wheels, a mountain (hanging tree, mawashiki, and trestle tree), and a belly (the front is a man's belly, and the rear is a woman's belly). During Shinto rituals, young people sit inside the boat and play instruments such as flutes and drums. === Hosha Festival === ``Obishasai'' is a special Shinto ritual held on March 17th. From the center of the worship hall, shoot the ``God's Arrow'' to the northeast and the ``Dono's Arrow'' to the southeast, then shoot the 12 feather arrows lowered into the Kagura Hall. It is said that the way the arrow hits the target predicts whether the year will be good or bad.History and Landscape of Hotaka Shrine, p. 12. . The ritual is designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Azumino City. === Cultural assets === === Cultural property designated by Nagano Prefecture === * Intangible folk cultural assets ** Customs of the Mifune Festival at Hotaka Shrine - Designated April 30, 2009 [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=5526&seq=0 Customs of the Mifune Festival at Hotaka Shrine] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . === Cultural property designated by Azumino City === *Tangible cultural property ** Washing Stone and Washing House (Stone Cultural Property) - Designated on October 29, 2008 [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4486&seq=0 Hotaka Shrine Hand Washing Stone and Washing House] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . ** Shinbashi (Stone Cultural Property) - Designated on October 29, 2008 [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4487&seq=0 Hotaka Shrine Shinbashi] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . ** 5 Sagi-ashi-zen tables at Hotaka Shrine (arts and crafts, etc.) - Designated on October 29, 2008 [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2667&seq=0 Hotaka Shrine's Sagi-ashi-zen tables] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . ** Sannomiya Hotakasha Gozogujo Nikki Volume 11 (calligraphy, classics, ancient documents) - A diary written from 1483 to 1585. Designated on October 29, 2008 (Heisei 20) [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2668&seq=0 Sannomiya Hotakasha Shrine Palace Diary] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . *Tangible folk cultural properties** 64 Ema Ema from Hotaka Shrine - Designated on October 29, 2008 [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4485&seq=0 Ema from Hotaka Shrine] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . * Natural monument ** Keyaki at the main gate of Hotaka Shrine - Designated on October 29, 2008 [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2689&seq=0 Keyaki at the main gate of Hotaka Shrine] (82 Cultural Foundation). . ** Hotaka Shrine Wakamiya West Keyaki - Designated October 29, 2008 [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4488&seq=0 Hotaka Shrine Wakamiya West Keyaki] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . * Intangible folk cultural assets ** Hosha Shinto Ritual at Hotaka Shrine - Designated on September 25, 2009 (Heisei 21) [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2689&seq=0 Hosha Shinto Ritual at Hotaka Shrine] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . ** Hotaka Shrine Shikinen Senza Festival - Designated September 25, 2009 (Heisei 21) [http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4844&seq=0 Hotaka Shrine Shikinen Senza Festival] (Hachijuni Cultural Foundation). . === Mifune Kaikan === Opened in 1982. It was established as a place to permanently introduce the Mifune and Gofurei rituals that characterize Hotaka Shrine, the Mifune collision, and the traditional Hotaka dolls, as well as explain the history of the Hotaka Shrine Sengu ritual and the rebuilding of the main shrine, as well as the Hotaka Shrine Okumiya and Minemiya [https://www2.kokugakuin.ac.jp/museum/jinja/16/16_hotaka.html Hotaka Shrine and Museum (Mifune Kaikan)] Kokugakuin University Graduate School. The building area is 760 {{ill|lt=square meters|Q25343|qid=Q25343|lt=m2|1=Square metre}}, the building structure is a two-story steel frame structure, and the collection is approximately 250 items. The main items in the collection include Mifune, Hotaka dolls, ancient documents (``Shinano Province Sannomiya Hotaka Shrine construction diary''), Dosojin, votive tablets, furniture, costumes, Hotaka doll decorations (historical picture scrolls), etc. . === Local information === '''Location''' * Motomiya: {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Azumino City|Q534667|qid=Q534667|1=Azumino}}Hodaka 6079 * Okumiya: Nagano Prefecture {{ill|lt=Matsumoto City|Q213324|qid=Q213324|1=Matsumoto, Nagano}} Azumi Kamikochi '''Transportation (to the main shrine)''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}} (JR East) {{ill|lt=Oito Line|Q385051|qid=Q385051|1=Ōito Line}} {{ill|lt=Hodaka Station|Q11597112|qid=Q11597112|1=Hotaka Station}} (about 3 minutes walk) * Highway bus: {{ill|lt=Chuo Expressway Bus|Q5119039|qid=Q5119039|1=Chūō Kōsoku Bus}} (Keio Bus/Alpico Kotsu) Right next to Azumino Hotaka bus stop. * Expressway: {{ill|lt=Nagano Expressway|Q6958441|qid=Q6958441|1=Nagano Expressway}} {{ill|lt=Azumino Interchange|Q11450772|qid=Q11450772|lt=Azumino IC|1=Azumino interchange}} '''Transportation (to Okumiya)''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Alpico Kotsu Kamikochi Line|Q10868354|qid=Q10868354|lt=Alpico Kotsu Kamikochi Line (commonly known as Matsumoto Electric Railway)|1=Kamikōchi Line}} From {{ill|lt=Shinshimashima Station|Q11502026|qid=Q11502026|1=Shinshimashima Station}} {{ill|lt=Matsumoto Electric Railway Bus|Q11531358|qid=Q11531358|lt=Bus|1=Matsumoto Dentetsu Bus}} about 1 hour and 5 minutes, get off at Kamikochi Bus Terminal and walk about 1 hour and 10 minutes. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist|2}} === References === * Shrine history book * “History and Landscape Hotaka Shrine” (Hotaka Shrine Office) * "{{ill|lt=Japanese Historical Place Name System|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} Place Names of Nagano Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) Hotaka Shrine Section, Minamiazumi District * Yoshimi Koana "Hotaka Shrine" ({{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} ed. "Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Places- 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano" {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}) === Related books === * {{ill|lt=Naoichi Miyaji|Q11453535|qid=Q11453535|1=Naokazu Miyaji}} "History of Hotaka Shrine" (Hotaka Shrine Office, 1949) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mr. Azumi|Q3377806|qid=Q3377806|lt=Mr. Azumi (Mr. Azumi)|1=Azumi people}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hirao Azumi|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Shigakai Shrine|Q11491171|qid=Q11491171|1=Shikaumi Shrine}} (Fukuoka City, Fukuoka Prefecture) - The birthplace of the Azumi clan. * Odatai Hachiman Shrine (Azumino City) - The former main hall of Hotaka Shrine was relocated in {{ill|lt=Kannobu|Q1145723|qid=Q1145723|1=Kan'en}}3 ({{ill|lt=1750|Q7611|qid=Q7611|1=1750}}). The oldest main hall of Hotaka-zukuri (a cultural property designated by Azumino City). * Gosha Shrine (Azumino City) - The former main shrine of Hotaka Shrine was relocated in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|qid=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}}12 ({{ill|lt=1829|Q7572|qid=Q7572|1=1829}}) (Azumino City designated cultural property). === External link === {{Commonscat|Hotaka jinja}} * [http://www.hotakajinja.com/ Hotaka Shrine] - Official website ** {{Instagram|hotakajinja}} * [http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/02cyusin/02minamiazumi/9021.htm Hotaka Shrine] - Nagano Prefectural Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/240301.html Hotaka Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Modern company ranking system}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hotaka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hotaka Shrine]][[Category:Regional Sōja]] [[Category:San-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shinano Province]] [[Category:Azumi people]] [[Category:Regional Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Hida Mountains]] [[Category:安曇野市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Azumino]]
